data.title: Client-Server Model
data.description: WhatsApp uses a client-server architecture to manage message transmission between users.
data.technical_spec: Messages are sent from the client app to WhatsApp servers which route them to the intended recipient.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Messages are encrypted client-to-client, ensuring only the communicating users can read them.
data.technical_spec: Uses the Signal Protocol to encrypt messages with keys stored only on user devices.

data.title: Multi-Device Support
data.description: Allows users to access messages and services from multiple devices simultaneously.
data.technical_spec: Synchronizes messages across devices without requiring the primary device to be online.

data.title: Media Transfer
data.description: Supports the transmission of multimedia messages, including images, videos, and voice notes.
data.technical_spec: Media is compressed, stored temporarily on servers, and downloaded onto devices when received.

data.title: Peer-to-Peer Connections
data.description: Facilitates direct connections between devices for certain types of communication.
data.technical_spec: Uses peer-to-peer connection strategies to optimize call quality.
